verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid non sapientia clamitat,  et prudentia dat vocem suam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלֹֽא־חׇכְמָ֥ה תִקְרָ֑א וּ֝תְבוּנָ֗ה תִּתֵּ֥ן קוֹלָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Doth not wisdom cry? and understanding put forth her voice?

verses.latin_vulgate: In summis excelsisque verticibus supra viam,  in mediis semitis stans,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּרֹאשׁ־מְרֹמִ֥ים עֲלֵי־דָ֑רֶךְ בֵּ֖ית נְתִיב֣וֹת נִצָּֽבָה׃
verses.king_james: She standeth in the top of high places, by the way in the places of the paths.

verses.latin_vulgate: juxta portas civitatis,  in ipsis foribus loquitur, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְיַד־שְׁעָרִ֥ים לְפִי־קָ֑רֶת מְב֖וֹא פְתָחִ֣ים תָּרֹֽנָּה׃
verses.king_james: She crieth at the gates, at the entry of the city, at the coming in at the doors.

verses.latin_vulgate: O viri, ad vos clamito,  et vox mea ad filios hominum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲלֵיכֶ֣ם אִישִׁ֣ים אֶקְרָ֑א וְ֝קוֹלִ֗י אֶל־בְּנֵ֥י אָדָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Unto you, O men, I call; and my voice is to the sons of man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Intelligite, parvuli, astutiam,  et insipientes, animadvertite.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָבִ֣ינוּ פְתָאיִ֣ם עׇרְמָ֑ה וּ֝כְסִילִ֗ים הָבִ֥ינוּ לֵֽב׃
verses.king_james: O ye simple, understand wisdom: and, ye fools, be ye of an understanding heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite, quoniam de rebus magnis locutura sum,  et aperientur labia mea ut recta prædicent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁ֭מְעוּ כִּֽי־נְגִידִ֣ים אֲדַבֵּ֑ר וּמִפְתַּ֥ח שְׂ֝פָתַ֗י מֵישָׁרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Hear; for I will speak of excellent things; and the opening of my lips shall be right things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Veritatem meditabitur guttur meum,  et labia mea detestabuntur impium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־אֱ֭מֶת יֶהְגֶּ֣ה חִכִּ֑י וְתוֹעֲבַ֖ת שְׂפָתַ֣י רֶֽשַׁע׃
verses.king_james: For my mouth shall speak truth; and wickedness is an abomination to my lips.

verses.latin_vulgate: Justi sunt omnes sermones mei :  non est in eis pravum quid, neque perversum ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּצֶ֥דֶק כׇּל־אִמְרֵי־פִ֑י אֵ֥ין בָּ֝הֶ֗ם נִפְתָּ֥ל וְעִקֵּֽשׁ׃
verses.king_james: All the words of my mouth are in righteousness; there is nothing froward or perverse in them.

verses.latin_vulgate: recti sunt intelligentibus,  et æqui invenientibus scientiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֻּלָּ֣ם נְ֭כֹחִים לַמֵּבִ֑ין וִ֝ישָׁרִ֗ים לְמֹ֣צְאֵי דָֽעַת׃
verses.king_james: They are all plain to him that understandeth, and right to them that find knowledge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accipite disciplinam meam, et non pecuniam ;  doctrinam magis quam aurum eligite :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קְחֽוּ־מוּסָרִ֥י וְאַל־כָּ֑סֶף וְ֝דַ֗עַת מֵחָר֥וּץ נִבְחָֽר׃
verses.king_james: Receive my instruction, and not silver; and knowledge rather than choice gold.

verses.latin_vulgate: melior est enim sapientia cunctis pretiosissimis,  et omne desiderabile ei non potest comparari.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־טוֹבָ֣ה חׇ֭כְמָה מִפְּנִינִ֑ים וְכׇל־חֲ֝פָצִ֗ים לֹ֣א יִֽשְׁווּ־בָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: For wisdom is better than rubies; and all the things that may be desired are not to be compared to it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego sapientia, habito in consilio,  et eruditis intersum cogitationibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲֽנִי־חׇ֭כְמָה שָׁכַ֣נְתִּי עׇרְמָ֑ה וְדַ֖עַת מְזִמּ֣וֹת אֶמְצָֽא׃
verses.king_james: I wisdom dwell with prudence, and find out knowledge of witty inventions.

verses.latin_vulgate: Timor Domini odit malum :  arrogantiam, et superbiam,  et viam pravam, et os bilingue, detestor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִ֥רְאַ֣ת יְהֹוָה֮ שְֽׂנֹ֫את־רָ֥ע גֵּ֘אָ֤ה וְגָא֨וֹן ׀ וְדֶ֣רֶךְ רָ֭ע וּפִ֨י תַהְפֻּכ֬וֹת שָׂנֵֽאתִי׃
verses.king_james: The fear of the Lord is to hate evil: pride, and arrogancy, and the evil way, and the froward mouth, do I hate.

verses.latin_vulgate: Meum est consilium et æquitas ;  mea est prudentia, mea est fortitudo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִֽי־עֵ֭צָה וְתוּשִׁיָּ֑ה אֲנִ֥י בִ֝ינָ֗ה לִ֣י גְבוּרָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Counsel is mine, and sound wisdom: I am understanding; I have strength.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per me reges regnant,  et legum conditores justa decernunt ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּ֭י מְלָכִ֣ים יִמְלֹ֑כוּ וְ֝רֹזְנִ֗ים יְחֹ֣קְקוּ צֶֽדֶק׃
verses.king_james: By me kings reign, and princes decree justice.

verses.latin_vulgate: per me principes imperant,  et potentes decernunt justitiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּ֭י שָׂרִ֣ים יָשֹׂ֑רוּ וּ֝נְדִיבִ֗ים כׇּל־שֹׁ֥פְטֵי צֶֽדֶק׃
verses.king_james: By me princes rule, and nobles, even all the judges of the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego diligentes me diligo,  et qui mane vigilant ad me, invenient me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲ֭נִי (אהביה) [אֹהֲבַ֣י] אֵהָ֑ב וּ֝מְשַׁחֲרַ֗י יִמְצָאֻֽנְנִי׃
verses.king_james: I love them that love me; and those that seek me early shall find me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mecum sunt divitiæ et gloria,  opes superbæ et justitia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֹֽשֶׁר־וְכָב֥וֹד אִתִּ֑י ה֥וֹן עָ֝תֵ֗ק וּצְדָקָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Riches and honour are with me; yea, durable riches and righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melior est enim fructus meus auro et lapide pretioso,  et genimina mea argento electo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֣וֹב פִּ֭רְיִי מֵחָר֣וּץ וּמִפָּ֑ז וּ֝תְבוּאָתִ֗י מִכֶּ֥סֶף נִבְחָֽר׃
verses.king_james: My fruit is better than gold, yea, than fine gold; and my revenue than choice silver.

verses.latin_vulgate: In viis justitiæ ambulo,  in medio semitarum judicii :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּאֹֽרַח־צְדָקָ֥ה אֲהַלֵּ֑ךְ בְּ֝ת֗וֹךְ נְתִיב֥וֹת מִשְׁפָּֽט׃
verses.king_james: I lead in the way of righteousness, in the midst of the paths of judgment:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut ditem diligentes me,  et thesauros eorum repleam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְהַנְחִ֖יל אֹהֲבַ֥י ׀ יֵ֑שׁ וְאֹצְרֹ֖תֵיהֶ֣ם אֲמַלֵּֽא׃
verses.king_james: That I may cause those that love me to inherit substance; and I will fill their treasures.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dominus possedit me in initio viarum suarum  antequam quidquam faceret a principio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְֽהֹוָ֗ה קָ֭נָנִי רֵאשִׁ֣ית דַּרְכּ֑וֹ קֶ֖דֶם מִפְעָלָ֣יו מֵאָֽז׃
verses.king_james: The Lord possessed me in the beginning of his way, before his works of old.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ab æterno ordinata sum,  et ex antiquis antequam terra fieret.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵ֭עוֹלָם נִסַּ֥כְתִּי מֵרֹ֗אשׁ מִקַּדְמֵי־אָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning, or ever the earth was.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nondum erant abyssi, et ego jam concepta eram :  necdum fontes aquarum eruperant,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּאֵין־תְּהֹמ֥וֹת חוֹלָ֑לְתִּי בְּאֵ֥ין מַ֝עְיָנ֗וֹת נִכְבַּדֵּי־מָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: When there were no depths, I was brought forth; when there were no fountains abounding with water.

verses.latin_vulgate: necdum montes gravi mole constiterant :  ante colles ego parturiebar.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּטֶ֣רֶם הָרִ֣ים הׇטְבָּ֑עוּ לִפְנֵ֖י גְבָע֣וֹת חוֹלָֽלְתִּי׃
verses.king_james: Before the mountains were settled, before the hills was I brought forth:

verses.latin_vulgate: Adhuc terram non fecerat, et flumina,  et cardines orbis terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַד־לֹ֣א עָ֭שָׂה אֶ֣רֶץ וְחוּצ֑וֹת וְ֝רֹ֗אשׁ עַפְר֥וֹת תֵּבֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: While as yet he had not made the earth, nor the fields, nor the highest part of the dust of the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quando præparabat cælos, aderam ;  quando certa lege et gyro vallabat abyssos ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּהֲכִינ֣וֹ שָׁ֭מַיִם שָׁ֣ם אָ֑נִי בְּחֻ֥קוֹ ח֝֗וּג עַל־פְּנֵ֥י תְהֽוֹם׃
verses.king_james: When he prepared the heavens, I was there: when he set a compass upon the face of the depth:

verses.latin_vulgate: quando æthera firmabat sursum,  et librabat fontes aquarum ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּאַמְּצ֣וֹ שְׁחָקִ֣ים מִמָּ֑עַל בַּ֝עֲז֗וֹז עִינ֥וֹת תְּהֽוֹם׃
verses.king_james: When he established the clouds above: when he strengthened the fountains of the deep:

verses.latin_vulgate: quando circumdabat mari terminum suum,  et legem ponebat aquis, ne transirent fines suos ;  quando appendebat fundamenta terræ :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּשׂ֘וּמ֤וֹ לַיָּ֨ם ׀ חֻקּ֗וֹ וּ֭מַיִם לֹ֣א יַֽעַבְרוּ־פִ֑יו בְּ֝חוּק֗וֹ מ֣וֹסְדֵי אָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: When he gave to the sea his decree, that the waters should not pass his commandment: when he appointed the foundations of the earth:

verses.latin_vulgate: cum eo eram, cuncta componens.  Et delectabar per singulos dies,  ludens coram eo omni tempore,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֶהְיֶ֥ה אֶצְל֗וֹ אָ֫מ֥וֹן וָאֶהְיֶ֣ה שַׁ֭עֲשׁוּעִים י֤וֹם ׀ י֑וֹם מְשַׂחֶ֖קֶת לְפָנָ֣יו בְּכׇל־עֵֽת׃
verses.king_james: Then I was by him, as one brought up with him: and I was daily his delight, rejoicing always before him;

verses.latin_vulgate: ludens in orbe terrarum ;  et deliciæ meæ esse cum filiis hominum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְ֭שַׂחֶקֶת בְּתֵבֵ֣ל אַרְצ֑וֹ וְ֝שַׁעֲשֻׁעַ֗י אֶת־בְּנֵ֥י אָדָֽם׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Rejoicing in the habitable part of his earth; and my delights were with the sons of men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc ergo, filii, audite me :  beati qui custodiunt vias meas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֣ה בָ֭נִים שִׁמְעוּ־לִ֑י וְ֝אַשְׁרֵ֗י דְּרָכַ֥י יִשְׁמֹֽרוּ׃
verses.king_james: Now therefore hearken unto me, O ye children: for blessed are they that keep my ways.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite disciplinam, et estote sapientes,  et nolite abjicere eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֖וּ מוּסָ֥ר וַחֲכָ֗מוּ וְאַל־תִּפְרָֽעוּ׃
verses.king_james: Hear instruction, and be wise, and refuse it not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Beatus homo qui audit me,  et qui vigilat ad fores meas quotidie,  et observat ad postes ostii mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֥שְֽׁרֵי־אָדָם֮ שֹׁמֵ֢עַֽ֫ לִ֥י לִשְׁקֹ֣ד עַל־דַּ֭לְתֹתַי י֤וֹם ׀ י֑וֹם לִ֝שְׁמֹ֗ר מְזוּזֹ֥ת פְּתָחָֽי׃
verses.king_james: Blessed is the man that heareth me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at the posts of my doors.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui me invenerit, inveniet vitam,  et hauriet salutem a Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י מֹ֭צְאִי (מצאי) [מָצָ֣א] חַיִּ֑ים וַיָּ֥פֶק רָ֝צ֗וֹן מֵיְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: For whoso findeth me findeth life, and shall obtain favour of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem in me peccaverit, lædet animam suam ;  omnes qui me oderunt diligunt mortem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽ֭חֹטְאִי חֹמֵ֣ס נַפְשׁ֑וֹ כׇּל־מְ֝שַׂנְאַ֗י אָ֣הֲבוּ מָֽוֶת׃
verses.king_james: But he that sinneth against me wrongeth his own soul: all they that hate me love death.
